macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Chair Powell Announces No Rate Change Amid Middle East Tensions and Inflation Pressures
items.summary: Jerome Powell stated the FOMC decided to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5% to 3.75%, citing solid economic expansion, resilient consumer spending, but weak housing and elevated PCE inflation at 3.5% driven by oil prices from Middle East conflict. The outlook remains uncertain with higher energy prices expected to push inflation higher.

items.title: US National Debt Surpasses 100% of GDP, Reaches Record $38.97 Trillion
items.summary: The US national debt held by the public exceeded 100% of GDP for the first time since WWII, totaling $38,967,833,861,543 as of May 1, 2026, amid projections of doubling in three decades due to spending. Gas prices rose to $4.392 per gallon and 30-year mortgage rates hit 6.3%.

items.title: Q1 GDP Grows 2%, Below Expectations; AI and Consumer Spending Drive Growth
items.summary: US first-quarter GDP expanded at 2%, slightly under the 2.3% forecast, supported by equipment investment in AI/computers and consumer spending. Economic activity remains solid but housing weak.

items.title: Powell Pledges to Remain as Fed Governor Post-Term, Rejects Shadow Chair Role
items.summary: Fed Chair Jerome Powell announced he won't retire after his term ends next month, committing to serve as a constructive governor without influencing as a shadow chair amid discussions on Fed independence.

items.title: Upcoming Key Data: Jobless Claims, Retail Sales, and PCE Prices Expected Today
items.summary: Markets await initial jobless claims (est. 215K), retail sales ex-autos (1.9%), PCE price index, and GDP revisions, with new home sales down 17.6% MoM. Fed speeches by Barr and Hammack scheduled.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Nears $78,000 as Crypto Market Cap Hits $2.6 Trillion
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at around $78,202, up 1.40% in the past 24 hours, with the total crypto market cap reaching $2.6 trillion amid strong ETF inflows of $1.9 billion. Ethereum climbed to $2,300 while Zcash jumped 8% daily.

items.title: Crypto Market Suffers $560M in Liquidations Amid US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Over $560 million in cryptocurrency positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours, linked to geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran. Separate reports noted $110 million in liquidations, primarily affecting Bitcoin and Ethereum long positions.

items.title: Cardano Trading Volume Surges 78% Despite Price Consolidation
items.summary: Cardano (ADA) saw a 78% jump in trading volume across major exchanges, signaling renewed market interest even as price remains range-bound between $0.24 and $0.25.

items.title: Visa Partners with Ripple Affiliate for BTC, ETH, XRP Rewards Cards in Japan
items.summary: Visa has teamed up with SBI Holdings, a Ripple affiliate, to launch credit cards offering rewards in Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP through SBI VC Trade.

items.title: Tom Lee’s Bitmine Acquires 10,000 ETH from Ethereum Foundation
items.summary: Bitmine, led by Tom Lee, purchased 10,000 ETH from the Ethereum Foundation, boosting its treasury and driving gains in BMNR stock.

items.title: Hyperliquid Launches Prediction Markets Upgrade on Mainnet
items.summary: Hyperliquid's prediction markets upgrade went live on mainnet, positioning it to compete with Polymarket and Kalshi.

items.title: Flare Founder: XRP Ledger Poised to Dominate RWA Issuance
items.summary: Flare's founder explained why the XRP Ledger could lead in real-world asset (RWA) issuance.

items.title: $2.1B Bitcoin and Ethereum Options Expire Today Amid Selloff Alerts
items.summary: Over $2.1 billion in Bitcoin and Ethereum options are set to expire, potentially increasing market volatility and selloffs.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Plans 10% Workforce Cut Tied to AI Investments
items.summary: Mark Zuckerberg announced Meta's plan to reduce its workforce by about 10% to fund increased AI investments, while not ruling out further layoffs. This reflects broader tech sector shifts toward AI infrastructure spending.[1][4]

items.title: Elon Musk Calls Himself 'Fool' for Backing OpenAI in Ongoing Lawsuit
items.summary: In his lawsuit against OpenAI, Elon Musk testified he felt like a 'fool' for funding the company, accusing Sam Altman of shifting from nonprofit mission to profit-driven model. The legal battle highlighted tensions over OpenAI's transformation.[1][4]

items.title: Google DeepMind Unveils AI Co-Clinician for Remote Healthcare Diagnostics
items.summary: Google DeepMind introduced an AI co-clinician capable of diagnosing conditions like acute pancreatitis, myasthenia gravis, and rotator cuff tendinitis via real-time video in telehealth, augmenting doctors by guiding exams and asking targeted questions.[5]

items.title: AI Powers Farm-ng Robots to Address Agriculture Labor Shortages
items.summary: California startup Farm-ng is using AI and robotics for seeding, weeding, and harvesting to tackle farm labor shortages and environmental impacts in agriculture.[4]

items.title: AI-Driven Deepfake Gang Bypasses Biometrics for Fraudulent Loans in India
items.summary: A gang in Ahmedabad used Google Gemini AI and deepfakes to circumvent Aadhar biometrics and OTPs for fraudulent loans, highlighting rising AI-powered crime risks.[3]

items.title: White-Collar Jobs Vulnerable to AI Replacement, Research Shows
items.summary: New research indicates white-collar workers face high risk of AI displacement, amid trends like reduced IT hiring at firms such as TCS and Cognizant in India.[3][4]

items.title: US Blocks Advanced AI Chip Tech Exports to China's Hua Hong
items.summary: The US has restricted advanced AI chip technology exports to China's Hua Hong amid concerns over national security and competition in AI development.[1]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto 6 Delayed to May 2026
items.summary: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto 6 to May 26, 2026, delivering the anticipated but dreaded news to fans.

items.title: Elder Scrolls: Oblivion Remake Officially Confirmed
items.summary: Bethesda has confirmed the Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion remake with a cryptic teaser, with a possible surprise reveal date coming sooner than expected; players are already discovering map glitches.

items.title: New Wheel of Time AAA RPG Announced
items.summary: A full-fledged AAA video game adaptation of The Wheel of Time is announced by a studio behind the hit live-action series, potentially rivaling Game of Thrones games.

items.title: PlayStation Plus Monthly Games for May Announced
items.summary: PlayStation Plus May games include EA Sports FC 26, Wuchang: Fallen Feathers, and Nine Sols, available starting May 5.

items.title: Nioh 3 Coming in 2026 with PS5 Demo
items.summary: Nioh 3 is set for release in 2026, and players can try its demo on PS5 right now.

items.title: 007: First Light Hands-On Report Released
items.summary: IO Interactive shares details on 007: First Light, highlighting stealth, action, and charm in the new James Bond game.

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles Launching September
items.summary: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles is scheduled for launch in September.

items.title: New Bloodstained Game Announced
items.summary: A new Bloodstained game follows Ritual of the Night from 2019.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Canonical's Ubuntu Servers Down After Sustained Cyberattack
items.summary: Servers operated by Ubuntu and parent company Canonical have been offline for over a day due to a sustained cross-border attack that began Thursday morning.

items.title: Critical cPanel Flaw CVE-2026-41940 Mass-Exploited in 'Sorry' Ransomware Attacks
items.summary: A newly disclosed cPanel vulnerability is being widely exploited to breach websites and deploy 'Sorry' ransomware, encrypting victim data.

items.title: Microsoft Releases Windows 11 KB5083631 Update with 34 Changes Including Xbox Mode
items.summary: The optional cumulative update for Windows 11 introduces Xbox mode for PCs, enhanced security for batch files, startup app performance improvements, and more.

items.title: Microsoft Tests Modern Windows Run Dialog with Dark Mode and Faster Performance
items.summary: Windows 11 preview build confirms a redesigned Run dialog featuring dark mode support and improved speed over the legacy version.

items.title: Nissan Abandons Plans for US EV Plant
items.summary: Nissan has scrapped its proposal to build a US electric vehicle factory that was set to produce 200,000 EVs annually.

items.title: Toilet Maker Toto Pledges Investment to Address NAND Memory RAM Crisis
items.summary: Toto, known for toilets, will invest more in its ceramics division to support NAND memory production amid ongoing RAM shortages.

items.title: Edu Tech Firm Instructure Discloses Cybersecurity Incident
items.summary: Instructure, maker of the Canvas learning platform, revealed a recent cyber incident and is investigating its potential impact.

items.title: ConsentFix v3 Attacks Target Azure with Automated OAuth Abuse
items.summary: A new automated attack variant, ConsentFix v3, is spreading on hacker forums, enhancing previous OAuth abuse techniques for scaling on Azure.
